blogs.title: Getting Started with React Router 7
blogs.content: Data routers combine routing with loaders and actions so data fetching stays colocated with the routes that need it.

blogs.title: Pastel UI and Readability
blogs.content: Soft palettes can keep long admin sessions comfortable without sacrificing hierarchy or contrast.

blogs.title: Draft: Upcoming Features
blogs.content: This post is still a draft and should only appear when drafts are included in filters.

blogs.title: React Router is Easy
blogs.content:
•    Declarative - enables basic routing features like matching URLs to components, navigating around the app, and providing active states with APIs like <Link>, useNavigate, and useLocation – demo: https://github.com/iproduct/course-node-express-react/tree/master/fmi-2026-10-react-router-declarative
•    Data - by moving route configuration outside of React rendering, Data Mode adds data loading, actions, pending states and more with APIs like loader, action, and useFetcher – demo: https://github.com/iproduct/course-node-express-react/tree/master/fmi-2025-react-router-v7-dataload
•    Framework - typesafe href, typesafe Route Module API, intelligent code splitting, SPA, SSR, and static rendering strategies

--------

•   Fulstack React Blogs Demo with React Router Datarouting, Material UI react components library, and json-server backed, including login/logout frontend mockup demo simulation – https://github.com/iproduct/course-node-express-react/tree/master/fmi-2026-11-react-router
